title: The Role of U.S. In Situ Ocean Observation Networks: A Case Study of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation
summary: A climate tipping point generally refers to a “critical threshold” in an Earth system that when crossed can cause the system to shift into a new state. Scientists contend that crossing a tipping point may result in a new long-term or permanent state from which the system may be unable to rebound, even if the trigger for crossing the threshold is reversed. One potential tipping point is the “collapse” (i.e.,...

title: How I Got My Career in Foreign Policy: Stephanie Psaki
summary: Stephanie Psaki has always believed that health is at the heart of every stage of life. She sat down with CFR to talk about her far-reaching career in international research, nonprofits, and the government, as well as her views on the current state of the public health space.

title: California’s Public Sector Unions Are Trying To Undo Pension Reform
summary: One step forward, three steps back.

title: Benjamon Jaros On The Fallout Over The Billionaire Tax Begins
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Benjamon Jaros discusses the fallout from California's proposed billionaire tax.

title: Orin Kerr: "So You're About To Start Law School"
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Orin Kerr offers incoming first-year law students tips for the first month of law school. Topics include: how to read an opinion, 1L course topics, the Socratic Method, how to avoid being a law school gunner, study groups, office hours, and avoiding comparisons with classmates.

title: Victor Davis Hanson: 2024 Proved Democrats Can’t Win An Honest Election
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson says if you look at population increases, there's no way in the world that you would get so many people voting in the US presidential election in 2020. Hanson notes the biggest issue with the 2020 election wasn't just allegations of fraud—it was how election rules changed under COVID-19. Hanson explains why those changes permanently altered the voting system, and he...

title: Prop 40 Says It Raises $100 Billion. Stanford Economist Benjamon Jaros Says California Loses Money.
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Benjamon Jaros discusses California's proposed billionaire tax which is supposed to raise $100 billion for healthcare, food assistance, and education, but Jaro's research at the Hoover Institution puts the real number closer to $40 billion — and argues the state could lose money overall once you factor in billionaires who've already left California.

title: House passes Pentagon funding bill and a blueprint to unlock new dollars for Iran war
summary: Republicans passed more than $1 trillion for the Pentagon alongside a budget blueprint to fund the war with Iran and implement provisions of President Trump's election overhaul bill.

title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
summary: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

title: People are watching the Reflecting Pool like reality TV. What does that say about us?
summary: It's not just watching paint dry. The twists and turns of the Reflecting Pool repairs, originally a two-week project, have kept bloggers and viewers busy all summer.

title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request at fiery hearing
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced a barrage of questions about the war with Iran from lawmakers on Tuesday, as the administration seeks more money for the escalating conflict.

title: Sen. Patty Murray, D-Wash., on Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's Senate testimony
summary: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Sen. Patty Murray of Washington about the administration's Iran war objectives following the testimony of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

title: House votes to adopt Department of War renaming as part of annual defense bill
summary: The House on Wednesday passed a defense policy bill that incorporates President Donald Trump's request for a historic $1.15 trillion in spending for national security and that would designate the Department of Defense as the Department of War.

title: Critics warn U.S. nuclear power deal with Saudi Arabia could fuel proliferation
summary: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia announced a landmark deal that would give the kingdom a civilian nuclear program using American technology, and might allow for uranium enrichment in Saudi Arabia. Critics warn it could lead to further nuclear proliferation. Nick Schifrin reports and Geoff Bennett discusses more with John Wolfsthal.

title: The implications of Trump loyalists and election deniers winning GOP primaries
summary: In Arizona, three prominent election deniers won Republican primaries for governor, secretary of state and attorney general. If they win in November, they'll play key roles in overseeing elections and defending voting laws. Amna Nawaz discussed more with Stephen Richer, the elections recorder in Maricopa County from 2021 to 2025.

title: Breakthroughs in Chinese AI models threaten U.S. lead in tech race
summary: Breakthroughs in Chinese AI models are sending shockwaves through Washington and Silicon Valley. Chinese companies are narrowing the AI gap despite vast differences in computing power and resources. It comes as the White House is planning to redirect billions in funding from universities to individuals in a bid to accelerate AI innovation. Geoff Bennett discussed more with Amrith Ramkumar.

title: 'This is about the rule of law': U.S. attorney fired by Trump sues to overturn dismissal
summary: A U.S. attorney in Washington state who was fired by the Trump administration is suing the government over his dismissal. Roger Rogoff was sworn in after a panel of judges unanimously selected him. Less than an hour later, he was fired via email. His lawsuit highlights the legal fight over Trump's power to shape the judiciary. Rogoff joined Justice correspondent Ali Rogin to discuss more.

title: Connecticut Democrats warn Yale risks tax status if it makes DOJ deal
summary: Connecticut Democratic lawmakers warned that Yale University's tax-exempt status is at risk if it decides to make a deal with the Department of Justice (DOJ) to resolve its investigations into the Ivy League school. University President Maurie McInnis last week said publicly that the school was speaking with the DOJ after it accused the Yale...

title: White House official accuses Chinese startup of distilling Anthropic model, accessing banned Nvidia chips
summary: A top White House official accused a Chinese startup on Wednesday of improperly using Anthropic's latest model and accessing restricted Nvidia chips to develop its new Kimi model. Michael Kratsios, the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, said the administration has information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic's Fable to produce...

title: Massachusetts voters to decide marijuana repeal question after challenge rejected
summary: Massachusetts voters will decide in November if legal marijuana will be repealed after a challenge to the proposal was rejected Tuesday. The State Ballot Law Commission overruled an objection to the petition seeking to end the Bay State's recreational cannabis law, allowing the proposal to be printed as a question on the ballot. The commission's...

title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities
summary: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.

title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
summary: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.

title: The Next Big Tests for the Left: Michigan, Wisconsin and Minnesota
summary: Progressives are now fighting in swingier terrain in purple or light-blue states.

title: Latest combat deaths show risk has shifted to troops far from Iran’s borders
summary: Trump flew to Delaware to honor soldiers killed in the Iran war, which has proved deadly for those far from the front lines.

title: Arizona primary takeaways: Trump’s picks prevail and a House Democrat survives
summary: Rep. Andy Biggs, who voted to overturn the 2020 election results, won the GOP nomination for governor. Among Democrats, a left-wing challenger failed to unseat Rep. Greg Stanton.

title: Mamdani says New York doesn’t have authority to arrest Netanyahu, urges feds to act
summary: The mayor had said the city might enforce a warrant against the Israeli leader, drawing Democratic criticism. On Tuesday, he urged the U.S. government to do so.

title: Democrats chip away at GOP cash lead centered on Trump
summary: Republicans stockpiled money from big donors in national groups heading into the fall campaign season, while individual Democrats have drawn on stronger voter enthusiasm.

summary: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
summary: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Tankers make sharp U-turns after Houthi shipping threat
summary: All of the ships were travelling to or from Saudi ports before changing course, ship-tracking data shows.

title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
summary: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

summary: Authorities are investigating the cause of the blaze in Lima as local media report it may have been arson.

title: Libya and U.S. Policy
summary: Fifteen years after a 2011 uprising and U.S.-backed military intervention that toppled longtime authoritarian leader Muammar al Qadhafi, Libya has yet to make a transition to stable governing arrangements. Elections and diplomacy have produced a series of interim governments, but militias, local leaders, and subnational coalitions backed by competing foreign patrons have remained powerful. Rival networks in western...

title: National Level Exercises: History, Authorities, and Congressional Considerations
summary: Every two years, the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) conducts a National Level Exercise (NLE) to evaluate the nation’s progress toward preparedness for catastrophic events. These exercises have multiple components, and culminate in a large, full-scale exercise with thousands of participants from multiple federal agencies, state, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments, industry partners, and...

title: An Overview of Small Business Contracting
summary: Congress has broad authority to impact the federal procurement process and how agencies purchase goods and services from private sector firms. One way in which Congress has exercised this authority is by adopting measures to promote contracts and subcontracts with “small businesses.” This report discusses federal contracting preferences for firms that meet the Small Business Administration’s definition of “small.”...

title: China's strategic petroleum reserve as a geoeconomic tool
summary: Chinese policy around the use of their SPR as a geoeconomic tool, particularly in crisis scenarios, is not well understood. This project investigates potential ways for China to do so, but not whether China actually intends to use it in this way.

title: To Base or Not to Base: The Iran War Exposes Flaws of U.S. Basing in the Middle East
summary: U.S. military installations across the Gulf sit within easy range of Iranian missiles, a legacy of basing decisions made when U.S. forces faced far less capable adversaries. As the war exposes that vulnerability, a case is building to reposition U.S. forces farther from Iran’s reach.

title: The AI Trilemma + Open-Weight Proliferation + the “Singularity Delusion”
summary: In conversation with Dan Kurtz-Phelan, Sebastian Mallaby discusses his recent Foreign Affairs article on the AI trilemma, and the push and pull U.S. leadership faces between innovating faster than China and addressing public concern about AI. Mallaby dismisses the race to build AGI first as a “singularity delusion,” saying the idea is held most strongly […]

title: Four Defense Export Reforms for the United States
summary: The global threat environment that the United States and its allies face in 2026 is darker than at any point since the Cold War, with military conflicts on three continents and menacing operations by Russia and the increasingly capable Chinese military. title: This Should Be the Next Enforcement Tool To Secure America’s Borders
summary: Praise is due to a recent Trump administration push to nail immigration lawyers for the broadly undeterred crime of running mass asylum fraud schemes, which have let huge volumes of ineligible illegal aliens into the country. The asylum system has long acted as a powerful magnet for aspiring illegal border crossers and visa overstayers, because, until President Donald Trump sharply curtailed its use, merely...

title: Trump meets with Lebanese leader, pledges support for peace efforts with Israel
summary: The event marked the White House’s latest attempt to broker an Israeli pullout from southern Lebanon, where Israel has been fighting Hezbollah.

title: Living with Wet Basements
summary: This report presents findings from a study conducted in Pittsburgh to document the prevalence of and experiences with basement wetness, the barriers that residents face in addressing it, and options available to solve the problem.

title: Examining the Impact of Senate Bill 1160 on Utilization Review and Medical Treatment in California Workers' Compensation
summary: Senate Bill 1160 reduced utilization review (UR) requirements for some treatments during the first 30 days after a work-related injury. The authors examined effects on UR processes and medical treatment in California’s workers’ compensation system.

title: Securing AI Algorithmic Insights
summary: In this report, the authors extend a security framework to provide the first systematic roadmap for protecting algorithmic insights, the novel techniques, methods, and design know-how that materially improve artificial intelligence systems.

title: Restricting AI Agent Use of Biological Tools
summary: AI agents can help non-experts use advanced biological design tools, raising misuse concerns. The authors explore safeguards built into biological tools that could block such assistance and find that their effectiveness is currently limited.

title: Another Hormuz? What to Know About the Houthi Threat to the Red Sea
summary: The Yemen-based Houthis’ decision to impose a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia opens a new front in the U.S.-Iran war and threatens to choke off the Red Sea’s Bab el-Mandeb Strait, with potentially major consequences for the global economy.

title: AllHere Chatbot Scandal Shows How Not to Deploy AI
summary: Want proof that policymakers and school staff need help with artificial intelligence? Look no further than the Los Angeles Unified School District. Superintendent Alberto Carvalho resigned June 21 as the district’s superintendent amid an FBI probe that media reports have linked to a failed contract for an AI chatbot. The episode is a case study of the need for greater transparency in public schools and the dangers...

title: How the U.S.-Europe Rift Got Balkanized
summary: Transatlantic disputes over trade, climate policy, defense spending, Iran, and free speech have certainly stressed the U.S.-Europe alliance. In the past, deft diplomacy has found ways to navigate those differences. Yet that comity is being severely tested as Europe takes steps to stifle American involvement in the Balkans. Last month, for the first time, Europe and the United States failed to agree on the next...

title: The Red-Blue Energy Divide: How Overregulation Inflates Prices
summary: Though recent events have made Americans in all parts of the country acutely aware of energy issues, stubbornly high prices in blue states illuminate policy challenges that have become impossible to ignore. Particularly, overly regulated California and New York face persistently high costs due to burdensome state-level energy policies. Residents are paying more because state governments have layered costly climate...

title: National Security Strategy (Joint Committee): Deterrence in an age of Russian aggression
summary: Armida van Rij gave evidence to UK Parliament's Joint Committee on the National Security Strategy for its inquiry into deterrence in an age of Russian aggression. View the whole committee meeting here . Armida van Rij View multimedia 13 July 2026

title: Budget Reconciliation Measures Enacted into Law Since 1980
summary: The budget reconciliation process is an optional procedure that operates as an adjunct to the budget resolution process established by the Congressional Budget Act of 1974. The chief purpose of the reconciliation process is to enhance Congress’s ability to change current law in order to bring revenue, spending, and debt limit levels into conformity with the policies of the annual budget resolution. summary: Armida van Rij, senior research fellow at the CER spoke with Jane Garvey on Times Radio to discuss the NATO summit and Donald Trump's remarks on his NATO allies. Armida van Rij View multimedia 08 July 2026

title: Unpacking Europe: The path to EU enlargement
summary: On this week’s Unpacking Europe podcast Zselyke Csaky sat down with Milan Nic a senior research fellow at the German Council on Foreign Relations (DGAP) to discuss the latest on EU enlargement.
